title: Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream
description: Rajas con Crema is a beloved Mexican vegetarian dish featuring smoky, tender poblano pepper strips bathed in a rich, creamy sauce. The gentle heat of the poblanos perfectly balances the luscious crema, creating a comforting and deeply flavorful experience that's both simple and profoundly satisfying.

ingredients.notes: or full-fat sour cream, thinned slightly with milk

instructions.title: Roast the Poblano Peppers
instructions.text: Place the poblano peppers directly over an open flame (gas burner), under a broiler, or on a hot comal/griddle. Roast, turning frequently with tongs, until the skin is completely charred and blistered on all sides. This 'tatemado' process should take about 8-12 minutes, ensuring an even char for maximum smoky flavor.

instructions.title: Sweat and Prepare the Rajas
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the hot, charred peppers to a bowl and cover tightly with plastic wrap, or place them in a paper bag and seal. Let them sweat for 15-20 minutes; this steaming helps loosen the skin. Once cooled enough to handle, peel off the charred skin, remove the stems and seeds, and slice the flesh into thin strips (rajas). Discard the skin, stems, and seeds.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large skillet or cazuela, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat. Add the thinly sliced white onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

instructions.title: Combine Peppers and Corn
instructions.text: Add the prepared poblano strips (rajas) and the corn kernels (if using) to the skillet with the sautéed aromatics. Sauté for 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the vegetables to warm through.

instructions.title: Add Crema and Simmer
instructions.text: Pour in the Crema Mexicana and the optional milk. Stir gently to combine everything, ensuring the rajas and corn are coated. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally, and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce slightly thickens and the flavors are fully incorporated. If using epazote, add the sprig now to infuse its unique herbaceous note.

instructions.title: Season and Serve
instructions.text: Season generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. If you added epazote, remove the sprig before serving. Serve hot as a side dish, filling for tacos, quesadillas, or sopes. This dish is best enjoyed immediately.

tips: For the best smoky flavor, ensure your poblanos are thoroughly charred on all sides before sweating. This 'tatemado' process is key to authentic rajas.
If Crema Mexicana is unavailable, a good quality full-fat sour cream, thinned with a little milk to achieve a pourable consistency, makes an acceptable substitute.
Rajas con Crema can be made a day ahead and gently reheated; the flavors often deepen overnight. Add a splash of milk or water if it's too thick when reheating.
article:
## History of Rajas con Crema Roasted Pepper Cream

Rajas con Crema is a classic dish rooted deeply in the culinary traditions of central Mexico, particularly prominent in regions like Puebla, Tlaxcala, and Mexico City. Its origins are humble, born from the availability of fresh poblano peppers and dairy products that were staples in Mexican households. The technique of roasting and peeling peppers, known as 'tatemar', is an ancient culinary practice in Mexico, used to enhance flavor, remove bitterness, and make the peppers more palatable. Over time, combining these smoky 'rajas' (strips) with rich, tangy crema became a beloved staple, offering a comforting and versatile dish that could be served as a side, a taco filling, or even a light main course. It exemplifies Mexican ingenuity in transforming simple, local ingredients into something extraordinary and enduring.

## Why This Recipe Works

This recipe for Rajas con Crema achieves its incredible depth and comforting appeal through the careful preparation and balance of its star ingredients. Roasting the poblano peppers is paramount; it imparts a crucial smoky flavor and softens their texture, making them tender and subtly sweet. The Crema Mexicana, with its distinct tangy and rich profile, forms the perfect luscious counterpoint to the peppers' mild heat and earthy notes. The gentle simmering allows all the flavors to meld beautifully, creating a harmonious and deeply satisfying sauce that coats every strip of poblano. The optional corn adds a touch of sweetness and textural contrast, making each bite a delightful balance of creamy, smoky, and subtly sweet elements.

## Chef Tips for Perfection

For truly authentic Rajas con Crema, never skip the proper roasting and sweating of the poblanos; this step is critical for developing their signature smoky flavor and tender texture. Ensure the peppers are fully charred before sweating, as this develops their crucial depth. When adding the crema, keep the heat low and simmer gently to prevent the sauce from breaking or curdling. Taste and adjust seasoning frequently – a good pinch of salt can elevate all the other flavors. If you can find fresh epazote, a small sprig simmered with the cream will impart an unmistakable, authentic herbaceous note that is truly Mexican and highly recommended for a traditional taste.

## Serving Suggestions

Rajas con Crema is incredibly versatile and can elevate almost any Mexican meal. It shines brightly as a flavorful side dish alongside grilled meats like carne asada or pollo asado. For a vegetarian meal, it makes an exquisite and satisfying filling for warm corn tortillas, creating simple yet profoundly delicious tacos. It's also fantastic as a topping for sopes, quesadillas, or even chilaquiles, adding a creamy, smoky layer of flavor. Don't hesitate to serve it alongside classic Mexican rice (arroz rojo) and refried beans (frijoles refritos) for a complete and comforting Mexican feast. It can even be stirred into scrambled eggs for a hearty and flavorful breakfast.

jsonLd.keywords: Mexican, Mexican, authentic, traditional, vegetarian, Mexican, authentic, traditional, vegetarian, poblano, crema
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Crema Mexicana (or full-fat sour cream, thinned slightly with milk)
1/2 cup Whole milk (optional, for desired consistency)
1/2 cup Fresh or frozen corn kernels (optional, but recommended)
to taste Freshly ground black pepper
